text:
Market today: Weak jobs (57K payrolls, unemployment 4.2%) is the real driver. Soft labor = rate-cut hope for some, but it also screams slowing growth. For tech/chips, that's a mixed bag - lower rates help valuations, but demand fears hurt semis most.

Your holdings: CRWD is the urgent one - down 72.79% and your stop is at 590 on a $195 stock, which looks like a data error; verify that stop immediately or you have no protection. SOXL is down 25.76% and already below its 200 stop - this leveraged 3X chip ETF is bleeding on a margin account, that's a real problem. SPAL is a new leveraged ETF already down 12.55% and trading near its 21.38 stop - one bad tick away from a forced decision.

Watch: Whether semis (SMH, SOXL) can hold current levels as weak jobs data filters into growth expectations. Also watch CRWD's stop level - it needs to be corrected.

Bottom line: Fix the CRWD stop error and decide on SOXL before the leveraged decay and margin pressure make the decision for you.
plays: CRWD: Your stop shows 590 on a stock at 195 - that stop is almost certainly wrong. Find the correct level and set it today; no margin position should run without a real stop.
SOXL: Already below its 200 stop at 188.93, down 25.76% on a 3X leveraged ETF held on margin. Seriously consider cutting or at minimum reducing size right now.
No new buys today - weak jobs data clouds the growth picture for semis, scanner setups are mostly grade B/C, and your existing positions need triage first.
